[CORRUPTED SPILLOVER - PARTIAL TEXT FROM PREVIOUS RECIPE]
blended mto the cream sauce. Season with lemon zest
and flakes of sea salt for brightness. Finish with a
generous knob of softened butter swirled through.

Chapter 7 | Classic French Fish Preparations
Page 42

The sauce should bubble gently for approximately ı5-ı8 minutes
until reduced by half. Pour over the prepared fish and bake
uncovered at 240°C/465-VI0 degrees.

Drizzle with aged balsamic before serving. Plate on a bed of
wilted greens or with buttered root vegetables as accompaniment.

---

BAKED COD WITH HERBS

Ingredients:
- 4 cod fillets (6 oz each), skin removed
- 3 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
- ½ cup dry white wine (or fish stock)
- 2 tablespoons unsalted butter
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 teaspoons fresh thyme
- 1 teaspoon fresh dill
- ½ teaspoon red pepper flakes
- Sea salt and black pepper
- Zest of 1 lemon
- Parchment paper for baking en papillote

Method:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C)
2. Tear 4 pieces parchment, place cod in center of each
3. Distribute garlic, herbs, salt, pepper over fish
4. Drizzle wine and lemon juice over each portion
5. Dot with butter
6. Fold parchment into sealed packets
7. Bake 15-18 minutes until fish flakes easily
8. Carefully open packets and serve with accumulated juices
